ucs.title: [LLM] Sidewinder (Rattlesnake/T-APT-04) CVE-2017-11882 Equation Editor exploitation chain from RTF lure
ucs.description: Hunts the Sidewinder hallmark of weaponised RTF spearphishing documents that exploit CVE-2017-11882 in Microsoft Equation Editor (EQNEDT32.EXE) to spawn LOLBin/scripting children — the primary initial-access pattern Sidewinder has used against South Asian government, military and diplomatic targets since 2017 and continues to use as recently as 2023-2024 against Pakistani and Nepalese government entities.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as image values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name="EQNEDT32.EXE" OR Processes.parent_process_name="eqnedt32.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","hh.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Sidewinder — EQNEDT32.EXE (CVE-2017-11882) spawning script/LOLBin child
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","hh.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "EQNEDT32.EXE"            // Equation Editor — only ever spawned by RTF exploit
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (_suspicious_children)
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","DownloadString","AppData\\Roaming","\\Temp\\")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent      = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentPath  = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentSHA256= InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          Child       = FileName,
          ChildPath   = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sidewinder — EQNEDT32.EXE child process via 4688
let _suspicious_children = dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","hh.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "EQNEDT32.EXE"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ChildBin = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where ChildBin in~ (_suspicious_children)
   or CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","DownloadString","AppData\\Roaming","\\Temp\\")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Sidewinder CVE-2017-11882 Equation Editor Child Process
id: 9b3d4f7e-1a2b-4c5d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects EQNEDT32.EXE spawning script interpreters or LOLBins, the canonical Sidewinder (T-APT-04 / Rattlesnake) RTF spearphishing initial-access chain.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0121/
  - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-11882
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.g0121
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\EQNEDT32.EXE'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
      - '\hh.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of Equation Editor is essentially nil since Microsoft pulled it in Jan 2018 — any child process is highly suspicious
level: high
ucs.rationale: EQNEDT32.EXE is an obsolete component that Microsoft removed from Office in Jan 2018 (CVE-2017-11882 patch). Its presence as a parent process is itself anomalous, and Sidewinder is one of a very small set of actors who continue to weaponise CVE-2017-11882 via RTF lures (Trend Micro 2018, Group-IB 2022, BlackBerry 2023, Kaspersky/Securelist 2024 reporting on Sidewinder against Pakistan/Nepal/Sri La

ucs.title: [LLM] Sidewinder mshta.exe staged HTA download chain from Office or scripting parent
ucs.description: Hunts Sidewinder's secondary initial-access pattern: weaponised LNK or Office documents invoking mshta.exe with a remote .hta URL, which then drops their JavaScript/VBScript loader (e.g. SideTwist, Backdoor.Agent.Sidewinder, ModuleInstaller). This chain has been used in 2022-2024 campaigns against Pakistani military, Maldivian government, and Nepali Foreign Ministry targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="mshta.exe" AND (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") OR Processes.parent_process_name="EQNEDT32.EXE") AND (Processes.process="*http*" OR Processes.process="*.hta*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=cmdline "(?i)(?<remote_url>https?://[^\"\s'>]+)" | where isnotnull(remote_url) | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Sidewinder — Office/script parent → mshta.exe pulling remote HTA
let _office_or_script_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","EQNEDT32.EXE"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName =~ "mshta.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_office_or_script_parents)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\.hta(\?|\s|$|"")"
| extend RemoteUrl = extract(@"(?i)(https?://[^\s""'>]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "mshta.exe"
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrlNet = RemoteUrl
  ) on DeviceId
| where isnull(NetTime) or NetTime between (Timestamp - 30s .. Timestamp + 5m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent     = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          MshtaCmd   = ProcessCommandLine,
          RemoteUrl, RemoteUrlNet, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel equivalent — relies on 4688 with command-line auditing
let _office_or_script_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","EQNEDT32.EXE"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ChildBin  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin == "mshta.exe"
| where ParentBin in~ (_office_or_script_parents)
| where CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://")
   or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\.hta(\?|\s|$|"")"
| extend RemoteUrl = extract(@"(?i)(https?://[^\s""'>]+)", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, RemoteUrl
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Sidewinder Office/Script Parent Spawning mshta.exe with Remote HTA URL
id: 7e2c5b6a-3f4d-4e9a-b1c2-d3e4f5a6b7c8
status: experimental
description: Detects mshta.exe spawned from Office, Explorer, or scripting hosts with a remote http(s) URL or .hta in the command line — Sidewinder's (G0121) standard staged loader pattern.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0121/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.g0121
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith: '\mshta.exe'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\EQNEDT32.EXE'
  selection_remote:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
      - '.hta'
  condition: selection_child and selection_parent and selection_remote
falsepositives:
  - Rare in-house management scripts that legitimately invoke mshta with a UNC or HTTP URL — should be allowlisted by hash/path
level: high
ucs.rationale: Sidewinder's signature staging chain since at least 2020 — public reporting from Group-IB, BlackBerry and Kaspersky consistently shows weaponised LNK / RTF / DOC documents launching mshta.exe to fetch a remote .hta JS/VBS stager (often using Cloudflare Workers, free dynamic DNS, or government-themed lookalike domains). Pairing the Office/scripting parent with the remote URL in the command line — a

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `RemoteUrl` is not a column of SecurityEvent; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.
